focus: Pin the exact implementation contract for a workflow-owned emitter step at `{artifact_dir}/resource-map.md`, then map the required auto/confirm YAML, command/docs, playbook/catalog, and edge-case updates.
answeredQuestions: How should the emitted review and research maps extend the phase-012 resource-map template without creating format drift or brittle template coupling?
Which command, YAML, docs, catalog, playbook, and test surfaces must change to satisfy the phase-013 acceptance criteria end-to-end?

summary: Template-faithful emission should preserve the existing four-column resource-map tables and encode review/research counts in Note or Summary fields instead of adding custom columns.
The workflow-owned emitter should be inserted in phase_synthesis after summary hydration and any confirm-mode pre-synthesis approval gate, but before research/report compilation.
Child-phase-safe emission must reuse the already-resolved artifact_dir from workflow state because resolveArtifactRoot allocates a new pt-NN folder on repeated child-phase resolves.
Phase-013 packet config wording should be revised from reducer-local snake_case resource_map.emit toward a workflow-owned config field consistent with the live camelCase JSON config schema.

The most important doc/test updates land in existing synthesis command docs, SKILL state-packet docs, synthesis feature-catalog files, and finalization playbooks; both playbook suites also carry stale 'no feature_catalog exists' notes that phase 013 should clean up.

approach: Adding custom review/research signal columns to the resource-map tables
reason: The locked phase-012 template standardizes the emitted table shape and category rules, so per-skill columns would create a deep-loop-only fork.

approach: Calling resolveArtifactRoot again inside the convergence emitter
reason: Repeated child-phase resolution can allocate a new sibling packet, so the emitter must inherit the already-resolved workflow artifact_dir.
